pi_items.source: task.json metadata and export_result.sh CHANNEL_NAME variable; also specified in task description
setup_task.sh line 85: docker exec nextgen-connect mkdir -p /opt/connect/outbound_hl7

HL7 standard documentation confirms ADT^A04 is a real message type for patient registration event. Verified via InterSystems Community, HL7 Definition (Caristix), and HL7 Definition standards at hl7-definition.caristix.com
setup_task.sh lines 47-72 contain exactly 25 INSERT statements, with MRN values from MRN-2024-00101 through MRN-2024-00125
HL7 standard - HL7 Version 2.5.1 is a real specification confirmed by HL7.org official implementation guides and multiple public health agency implementations (CDC, state health departments)
GitHub nextgenhealthcare/connect releases confirm Mirth Connect 4.5.0 and 4.5.2 releases are real. This was the last open-source version before proprietary licensing started with 4.6

setup_task.sh contains hardcoded INSERT statements with generated patient data. No external downloads or API calls for data. All records have synthetic MRNs (MRN-2024-XXXXX), common US names, and realistic addresses from major US cities
task.json description specifies the exact JDBC URL for PostgreSQL connection
setup_task.sh uses 'mirthdb' database and task description specifies JDBC URL with /mirthdb

privileged_info_summary: The task deploys a Mirth Connect 4.5 channel named 'DB_Patient_Registration_Feed' that reads from a PostgreSQL database table 'patient_registrations' containing 25 synthetic patient records (with realistic names and US addresses, MRNs in format MRN-2024-00101 to MRN-2024-00125). The channel converts database records to HL7 v2.5.1 ADT^A04 messages (a real HL7 message type used for patient registration events) and writes them as .hl7 files to the output directory /opt/connect/outbound_hl7 inside the nextgen-connect container. All patient data is synthetic and generated within the setup script with no external data sources. The task uses PostgreSQL JDBC connection to jdbc:postgresql://nextgen-postgres:5432/mirthdb with standard credentials (admin/admin for Mirth Connect API).
